steps.title: Identify the strategic incentive
steps.content: The key clue is that rural women deliberately sell less frequently to increase profits per unit. This implies a cost structure where reducing frequency improves per-unit margins.

steps.title: Match the cost structure
steps.content:
Choice D shows how this works: transportation costs are high but independent of quantity.

Consolidating fewer, larger trips spreads that fixed cost across more units, reducing per-unit cost and increasing profit per unit.

The other choices don't explain how reduced frequency improves per-unit economics.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
